data.title: Moldova moves to regulate crypto under EU-style MiCA rules
data.description:
Moldova plans to introduce its first crypto law in 2026, aligning with the EU’s MiCA framework while warning citizens about digital asset risks.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/moldova-legalize-crypto-2026-eu-mica-regulations?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Moldova plans to introduce crypto legislation by the end of 2026, which will align with the EU's MiCA framework.
data.description: Moldovan Finance Minister Andrian Gavrilita stated that Moldova plans to introduce its first systemic cryptocurrency legislation by the end of 2026, aligning its regulatory framework with the EU's Crypto-Assets Market Regulation (MiCA). The law will allow citizens to legally hold and trade crypto assets, but will not recognize them as legal tender. Gavrilita noted that the government is working with the central bank, financial market regulators, and anti-money laundering agencies to develop the legal framework to fulfill its commitment to align with the EU. He emphasized that crypto assets should be viewed as a highly volatile speculative area rather than a traditional investment, but citizens will still have the right to participate in related activities within compliance limits. The report states that this legislation will be Moldova's first formal cryptocurrency legal framework. Previously, the Central Bank of Moldova had repeatedly warned of the price volatility and money laundering risks associated with crypto assets. This legislative advancement comes against the backdrop of the EU's MiCA, which came into full effect at the end of 2024, becoming Europe's first unified regulatory framework covering the cryptocurrency industry. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Controversial Economist Issues New Bitcoin Crash Warning
data.description:
Longtime gold advocate and Bitcoin (BTC) critic Peter Schiff is once again issuing a stark warning to investors, and this time, he says major moves are coming across both precious metals and cryptocurrency markets. Specifically, in a post shared on X on January 16, Schiff warned that investors need to prepare for significant volatility ahead. com
source: https://www.techgaged.com/controversial-economist-issues-new-bitcoin-crash-warning/

data.title: Binance Announces DOLO Flexible Earn Promotion with Up to 30% APR
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has launched a promotion for DOLO Simple Earn Flexible Products, offering users the opportunity to earn up to 30% APR during the specified promotion period. This promotion is set to run from 2026-01-17 00:00 (UTC) to 2026-03-17 23:59 (UTC). Users who subscribe to DOLO Flexible Products can benefit from both Real-Time APR and Bonus Tiered APR rewards. The subscription process follows a first-come, first-served basis, with rewards distributed daily to users' Spot Accounts. The Real-Time APR is accrued every minute and directly accumulated in users' Earn Accounts.

The promotion details specify that the DOLO Flexible Product offers a 30% APR, which includes approximately 15% Bonus Tiered APR and 15% Real-Time APR. Users can subscribe with a minimum of 0.1 DOLO and a maximum of 500,000 DOLO. The Bonus Tiered APR rewards begin accruing the day after subscription and are distributed the following day between 00:00 (UTC) and 08:00 (UTC). Users holding open positions for DOLO Flexible Products will receive both types of APR rewards during the promotion period, but only Real-Time APR rewards after the promotion ends. Subscription limits apply, and once reached, further subscriptions will be halted.

Binance emphasizes that only users who complete identity verification during the promotion period are eligible for rewards, and sub-accounts do not qualify for Bonus Tiered APR rewards. The platform reserves the right to amend terms and conditions without prior notice, including changes to the rewards rate. Users are advised to refer to Binance Simple Earn Terms & Conditions and Risk Warning for more information. Redemption requests may face temporary delays due to liquidity issues, but redemption time for Flexible Products subscriptions is instant. Binance maintains the right to disqualify users involved in dishonest behavior or tampering with program code.

data.title: Singapore-based cryptocurrency platform Veera completes $10 million funding round
data.description: Singapore-based crypto financial services platform Veera announced it has raised a total of $10 million in Pre-seed and Seed funding rounds. Investors in this round include Sigma Capital, CMCC Titan Fund, 6th Man Ventures, and Ayon Capital. Veera stated that the funds will primarily be used for product development and further expansion of its on-chain financial services. The company launched its mobile-centric platform in January 2025, and it has accumulated over 2 million downloads with approximately 220,000 monthly active users. Veera supports self-custodied wallet functionality and has launched a Veera Card waiting list, planning to provide users with global on-chain asset payment capabilities. (Tech in Asia)

data.title: Bitcoin Removed from Portfolio Amid Quantum Computing Concerns
data.description: According to Foresight News, Christopher Wood, the global head of equity strategy at Jefferies, has eliminated the 10% Bitcoin allocation from his model portfolio. Wood expressed concerns that the rapid advancement of quantum computing poses a potential threat to Bitcoin's encryption security and its status as a long-term store of value, particularly for pension investors seeking stability. The removed Bitcoin allocation has been redistributed into the gold sector, with 5% allocated to physical gold and another 5% to gold mining stocks. Wood noted that since the initial allocation in December 2020, Bitcoin has surged by 325%, significantly outperforming gold, which rose by 145% during the same period. However, amid rising geopolitical risks and emerging technological threats, gold's historical role as a safe haven is becoming increasingly attractive.

data.title: Texas real estate developer Megagatel has received a "no action" letter from the SEC to launch a crypto rewards token.
data.description: Texas-based private residential developer Megatel Homes announced plans to launch "MegPrime," a cryptocurrency token for payments and rewards, in the coming weeks. This decision follows a "no action" letter from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), meaning the regulator will not take enforcement action against the project. Megatel CEO Zach Ipour stated that MegPrime marks the company's first foray into digital assets, and the token will serve solely as a payment and rewards tool, not an investment product. Consumers can use the token at regular merchants via digital wallets and payment cards, earning cashback, discounted gift cards, and points that can be used for housing-related benefits. The report notes that MegPrime does not grant holders any securities-related rights, such as voting rights or profit-sharing rights. Megatel believes that this model, combined with its real estate development and financing business, could further integrate real estate activities with digital currency incentive mechanisms. (Reuters)

data.title: Ethereum Shows Signs of Long-Term Recovery Amid Strong Demand
data.description: CyrilXBT posted on X. Ethereum (ETH) is demonstrating signs of validating a long-term bottom thesis, with a strong reaction observed from the generational demand zone. The cryptocurrency is forming higher highs and higher lows during its recovery phase, indicating a bullish trend. Currently, the price is compressing below resistance levels, while buyers are consistently defending against pullbacks. This activity suggests a positive outlook for Ethereum's future performance.

data.title: CoinGecko's Valuation and Revenue Streams Analyzed
data.description:
Stacy Muur posted on X about the valuation of CoinGecko, which is currently estimated at $500 million. CoinGecko attracts approximately 18.4 million visitors monthly, and its advertising revenue is a significant contributor to its earnings. Ads typically sell for $8-$10 per 1,000 impressions, and with three ad slots, CoinGecko could earn around $500,000 per month, translating to an annual revenue of approximately $6 million.

However, CoinGecko's revenue is not solely dependent on advertising. The company also benefits from alternative revenue sources such as premium listings and animated icons. These additional streams suggest that CoinGecko's annual revenue might be closer to $10-12 million.

Despite its substantial valuation, CoinGecko's $500 million price tag is considered reasonable when compared to other Web3 companies, some of which are trading at valuations exceeding $1 billion. The attention CoinGecko commands allows it to explore new product offerings, potentially enhancing its market position further.

data.title: Iran Protests Drive Bitcoin Rush as Rial Suffers Historic Collapse
data.description:
Iranians are rapidly shifting savings into Bitcoin as the national currency spirals and civil unrest intensifies. Protests that began on December 28, 2025, have evolved into a broad regime-change movement, met by internet blackouts and mass arrests, pushing households to seek financial lifelines outside the domestic banking system.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/iran-protests-drive-bitcoin-rush-as-rial-suffers-historic-collapse/

data.title: Investor Sees Significant Profit from GAS Token Transactions
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Lookonchain monitoring has revealed that an investor spent $394 to acquire 12.6 million GAS tokens. The investor has sold 5.3 million GAS tokens, earning $98,800, and currently holds 7.3 million GAS tokens. The total profit from these transactions is approximately $420,700, with a return on investment of 53,542%.

Foresight News advises caution when investing in meme tokens due to their high volatility.
